relocated_functions.note: Function moved from resolver to service. Functionally present but wrong receiver.

extra_go_functions.python_equivalent: None on Service — Python has Controller.export()

signature_mismatches.python: KeyController.export(destination: Path, *, keys_dir: Path, overwrite: bool = False) -> tuple[Path, Path]
signature_mismatches.go: Controller.Export(ctx context.Context, destDir string, overwrite bool) (string, string, error)
signature_mismatches.diff: keys_dir param removed — stored in struct at construction time. ctx added. Returns paths as strings.

signature_mismatches.python: KeyService.get_pubkey(self, key: str|SSHKeyItem, keys_dir: Path) -> str
signature_mismatches.diff: keys_dir param removed — stored in struct. ctx added. Returns error.

signature_mismatches.python: KeyService.get_pubkeys(self, keys: list[str]|list[SSHKeyItem], keys_dir: Path) -> list[str]
KeyService.list_all(self, keys_dir: Path, *, verify: bool = True) -> list[SSHKeyItem]
signature_mismatches.diff: keys_dir param removed. ctx added. Returns error.

signature_mismatches.python: KeyService.add_key(self, name, pub_key_path, pub_key_content, keys_dir, *, overwrite) -> SSHKeyItem
signature_mismatches.go: Service.AddKey(ctx, name, pubKeyPath, pubKeyContent, overwrite) (*SSHKeyItem, error)

signature_mismatches.python: KeyService._generate_keypair(self, private_key_path, pub_key_path, comment, algorithm='ed25519', bits=None) -> str
signature_mismatches.go: Service.generateKeypair(ctx, privateKeyPath, pubKeyPath, comment, algorithm string, bits int) (string, error)
signature_mismatches.diff: ctx added. bits defaults to 4096 inside function body instead of via param default.
No receiver. Returns error instead of raising exception.

signature_mismatches.python: KeyService._parse_algorithm(self, pub_key_content) -> str (raises MVMKeyError if empty)
signature_mismatches.diff: No receiver. Returns error instead of raising exception. Exported not private.
No receiver. Exported not private. Whitespace handling differs (split(None,2) vs Fields+Join).

error_code_mismatches.go: returns fmt.Errorf("invalid public key format") — untyped, not a DomainError

error_code_mismatches.issue: export not found error message case

error_code_mismatches.python: "Key file(s) already exist: ... Use --overwrite to replace."

sql_query_mismatches.issue: All SELECT queries use SELECT * in Python, explicit columns in Go
sql_query_mismatches.go: SELECT id, name, fingerprint, algorithm, comment, private_key_path, public_key_path, is_default, is_present, created_at, updated_at FROM ssh_keys WHERE name = ?
sql_query_mismatches.severity: MEDIUM — behaviorally identical but violates Rule 5

behavioral_differences.go: Service.SetDefaultKeys() returns aggregated error if any SetDefault() fails

behavioral_differences.python: KeyService._parse_comment uses split(None, 2) — preserves original whitespace in comment
behavioral_differences.go: ParseComment uses strings.Fields + Join — normalizes all whitespace to single space

summary: All 41 Python functions have Go equivalents (0 truly missing), but the port has: 4 extra Go functions with no Python equivalent, 1 function relocated to wrong receiver, 9 signature mismatches, 4 error code/discrepancy issues, SQL SELECT * → explicit columns in 4 queries, and 3 behavioral differences. Two conflicting KeyFileError definitions create confusion about the intended error code for file I/O errors. The set_default_keys behavioral difference (silent skip → aggregated error) is the most impactful caller-facing change.
